steps.title: Turn the slope into a second point
steps.content: The line has slope $-\frac{1}{2}$ and passes through $(6,4)$. Move $2$ units right and $1$ unit down to get another point on the line: $(8,3)$.

steps.title: Solve for the y-intercept and write the equation
steps.content:
Use $y=mx+b$ with $m=-\frac{1}{2}$ and point $(6,4)$:
$$4=-\frac{1}{2}(6)+b$$
$$4=-3+b$$
$$b=7$$
The equation is $$y=-\frac{x}{2}+7.$$ This matches, so D is correct.
